title: Write a book
note: Two ML books published with Packt: Mobile Artificial Intelligence Projects and Machine Learning for Mobile Applications.

title: Meet Thalaivar Rajinikanth
note: The man, the myth, the punchlines. Someday, somewhere — preferably without a queue.

title: 1M downloads on a personal app
note: Teleport Call hit 420k organic downloads in the first month — close, but not crossed yet on anything I solely owned.

title: Manage a large engineering team
note: Multiple times across nference, Sequoia, Chumbak, Pi42, VisionPlus.

title: Start my own company
note: GazeMatic — Teleport Call VR app. Shipped, hit 420k+ downloads, ran for 4 years. Didn't reach escape velocity. Counts.

title: Skydive
note: Dubai, 13,000 feet, free fall. The 60 seconds of weightlessness rewires you a little.

title: Helicopter trip
note: Some valley, some coastline, some sunrise. Not picky.

title: Set foot in Antarctica
note: The seventh continent. Probably via a research vessel from Ushuaia.

title: Watch the Northern Lights
note: Tromsø, Reykjavik, Yellowknife — anywhere the aurora puts on its show.

title: Dip in ice-cold running water
note: Glacier-fed stream, the kind of cold that makes you forget your name for a few seconds.

title: Grow my own coconut trees
note: Plant them young, watch them outlive me. Madurai backyard, ideally.

title: Build a wooden farmhouse
note: Slow-living escape, off-grid solar, a porch big enough for a hammock.

title: Win 20 hackathons
note: Two more to go. Specifically scoped to hackathons I personally led or co-led, not team participations.

title: Ship a personal hack that goes viral
note: A weekend-built thing that hits the front page and stays useful past the news cycle.

title: Own a 7-seater SUV
note: On the list more out of family-trip optionality than need. Currently no need — re-evaluating.

title: Run an LLM on a local home machine
note: Currently experimenting with the Dailyvox app. Llama 3 + a beefy Mac — closing in.

title: Possess a rare unique item on Earth
note: Open-ended on purpose. A first edition, a meteorite, a one-of-one piece — TBD.

title: Guinness World Record
note: Was part of a team that set a Guinness record at a Microsoft hackathon. Counts as a partial — doing one solo or as the lead is the next step.

title: Multi-country trip in one go
note: A proper itinerary — three or more countries, two-plus weeks, one passport.

title: Scuba dive
note: Multiple times. Coral reefs, low-vis wrecks, night dives. The ocean is louder than you'd think.

title: Earn a PhD
note: Probably mid-life, probably in something at the intersection of distributed systems and applied ML. No rush.

title: Sell at a shop in a foreign country
note: Spend a week behind a counter abroad — different language, different currency, real customers. Learn the trade by doing it.

title: Dance on stage in front of an audience
note: Done twice. Stage lights, live audience, the kind of nerves that make you forget the count for the first eight beats.

title: Complete 50+ treks
note: Roughly 40 trails behind me — Western Ghats, Himalayas, Sahyadris. Ten more before I hit half a hundred.

title: Cook for a large audience
note: A proper feast for 50+ guests. Menu planned end-to-end, ingredients sourced, no caterers — just a long apron and a sharp knife.

title: Volunteer for a bigger cause
note: Sustained, hands-on, not just a donation. Pick something that compounds — education, disaster relief, environment — and show up for years.

title: Learn a musical instrument
note: Pick one — guitar, keyboard, mridangam — and stay with it long enough that it stops sounding like a fight.

title: Ride a long-haul convoy trip
note: Multi-day road convoy with friends — bikes or cars, single arrow on the map, fuel stops as the only forced pauses.

title: Build a home AI assistant like Jarvis
note: A personal AI that runs the house — voice-controlled, context-aware, integrated with lights, security, calendar, trading alerts. Iron Man's Jarvis, but real and self-hosted.
